name: Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park Campground
location: Big Sur — Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park
highlights: 218 sites in a cathedral redwood grove along the Big Sur River
Most popular campground in California
Dark sky conditions good
River provides constant ambient sound
Walking distance to every trailhead
bookingWindow: Reservations open 6 months in advance at reservecalifornia.com — book within minutes of release
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Set a reservation alert — fills instantly for peak season.

name: Kirk Creek Campground
location: Big Sur — Los Padres National Forest (southern corridor)
highlights: Clifftop sites directly above the Pacific
Most dramatic camping in California
33 sites, all with ocean views
No hookups, no showers — National Forest land
Among the best drive-in stargazing campgrounds in California
bookingWindow: Reservations via recreation.gov — book 6 months out
seasonalNotes: Year-round. 30 miles south of Pfeiffer — the southern Big Sur corridor.

name: Ventana Campground / Glampsites
location: Big Sur — Alila Ventana Big Sur property
highlights: Safari-style canvas glampsites in a 40-acre redwood canyon
15 glampsites with custom mattresses, heated blankets, private fire pits
Bathhouse access with teak showers and marble vanities
Traditional tent camping also available in the same canyon
bookingWindow: Book well in advance for summer — limited availability
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Glampsites are the middle path between camping and the full Alila resort.

name: Treebones Resort
highlights: Yurt resort perched on a coastal ridge above the Pacific
16 yurts with ocean views
Pool, hot tub, nightly s'mores by the fire
The Human Nest — a bookable sculptural outdoor sleeping structure
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The Human Nest is a singular experience — book early.

name: Glen Oaks Big Sur
highlights: Cabins and motor lodge rooms in the redwoods alongside the Big Sur River
Each unit is different — clawfoot tubs, wood-burning fireplaces
Design-forward, sustainably built
Natural wool and Coyuchi organic cotton
Walking distance to Big Sur Bakery and trailheads
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Each cabin is unique — request details when booking.

name: Big Sur Lodge
location: Big Sur — Inside Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park
highlights: Operated by California State Parks
Cottages inside the state park
Walking distance to every trailhead
Least expensive roofed accommodation in the Big Sur corridor
Most convenient for serious hikers
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Basic but storied — the trailhead access is unmatched.

name: Fernwood Resort
highlights: Full-service community hub of Big Sur
Motel rooms, cabins, glamping tents, campground
Fernwood Tavern and general store on-site
The most social place to stay on the coast
bookingWindow: Book 2–3 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Nothing exceptional individually, but the sum is more than its parts.

name: Asilomar State Beach & Conference Grounds
highlights: 107 acres of Pacific Grove beachfront
30 historic buildings designed by Julia Morgan (architect of Hearst Castle)
No TVs or phones in rooms
Fire pits, heated pool, walking paths through pines and dunes
Walking distance to Monarch Butterfly Sanctuary and 17-Mile Drive
Chronically underrated
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The fireplace-and-fog atmosphere makes you stay another night.

name: L'Auberge Carmel
highlights: Relais & Châteaux property in a 1929 building
20 rooms with antique furnishings and fireplaces
Aubergine restaurant (one Michelin star) downstairs
The right base for exploring Carmel and Big Sur
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Intimate and refined — 20 rooms total.

name: Bernardus Lodge & Spa
highlights: Vineyard estate in Carmel Valley wine country
Full-service spa, heated pool, croquet lawns
Lucia restaurant built around estate wine and local produce
Warm, dry, sun-drenched when the coastal fog is thick
Big Sur and Carmel a 20-minute drive away
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The counterpoint to the coast — warm when the fog rolls in.

name: Post Ranch Inn
highlights: The most acclaimed hotel on the California coast
40 accommodations — ocean suites, treehouses, cliff houses cantilevered 1,200 ft above the sea
No TVs or alarm clocks; no children under 18
Breakfast, daily yoga, guided hikes, stargazing, forest meditation included
Sierra Mar restaurant; Three Michelin Keys (2025)
#1 Top Hideaway in the US (Andrew Harper, 2 consecutive years)
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 months ahead; longer for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The standard against which all similar experiences are measured.

name: Alila Ventana Big Sur
location: Big Sur — Redwood canyon above the Pacific
highlights: Adults-only, all-inclusive resort
All meals, beverages, wellness programming, Spa Alila access included
Japanese-inspired hot baths and outdoor pools
Guided experiences and forest bathing walks
Sur House restaurant open to non-guests
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. All-inclusive means no surprise costs — everything is in the rate.

name: Hyatt Carmel Highlands
location: Carmel Highlands (10 miles south of Carmel)
highlights: Cliffside above Big Sur and Carmel
Panoramic ocean views, fireplaces in most rooms
Infinity pool
Less stratospheric price than Post Ranch with similar view quality
bookingWindow: Book 2–3 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Strong base for exploring both Carmel and Big Sur corridor.
